title: Decal Object
description: Represents a Decal within a component.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the decal from the component.

methods.name: redefine
methods.description: Redefines the position, orientation, and how the decal is applied to the body.   To call this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this decal. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisDecal.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

methods.name: saveImage
methods.description: Saves the image associated with the decal to the specified file. This is useful in cases where the original image file is no longer available but you need the image for some other purpose.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: deriveFeature
properties.description: Returns the DeriveFeature if this decal is derived from another design. This property returns null if the decal is not derived from another design (i.e. isDerived property returns false).

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Decal object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same Decal.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: faces
properties.description: Gets the faces the decal is associated with. Typically, this is an array containing a single face. If the isChainFaces property is true, this will return the primary face. If the isChainFaces property is false, the decal is limited to the faces in this list.   If multiple faces have been provided, the first face in the list is the primary face, which is used to position and orient the decal.   To set the faces, use the redefine method.

properties.name: imageFilename
properties.description: Gets and sets the filename of the image used for the decal. When getting this property, the filename returned is the file that was used when the decal was initially created. it's possible the file may no longer exist.   When setting this property, it is the full filename to the image to use for the decal. PNG, JPEG, and TIFF files are supported.

properties.name: isChainFaces
properties.description: Returns if the decal is limited to a specified set of faces or wraps onto all faces in the body. If this property is True, a single face has been specified and the decal can wrap onto other faces of the body. If False, the decal is limited to the set of specified faces.   To change this setting, use the redefine method.

properties.name: isDerived
properties.description: Returns if this decal is derived from another design. If true, the canvas cannot be deleted. You should not attempt to make any edits to the derived decal. Any edits made to this derived decal will be lost when the derive updates.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of this decal as displayed in the browser is on or off.   A decal will only be visible if the light bulb is switched on. However, the light bulb can be on and the decal still invisible if the visibility of a higher level occurrence has its light bulb off or if the light bulb for the Decals folder is off to turn off all decals in a component.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Returns if the decal is currently visible in the graphics window. The isLightBulbOn property of the decal controls if the decal should be displayed or not, but even when true, the decal may not be visible because the occurrence that references the component may not be visible. It's also possible to turn off the visibility of all the decals in a component. This property takes all of that into account when reporting if the decal is visible or not.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the decal. This is the name seen in the browser and timeline.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of it's parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: opacity
properties.description: Gets and sets the opacity of the decal where 0 is completely transparent and 1.0 is completely opaque. Setting this property to a value outside the range of 0-1 will result in the value being set to the closest valid value.   Defaults to 1.0 when the input is created.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with the creation of this decal.

properties.name: transform
properties.description: Gets the transform of the decal. The returned matrix defines the position, rotation, scaling, and flipping. This is done by providing a 3D matrix which defines a 3D coordinate system in model space. The origin of the matrix defines the center of the decal and must lie somewhere on the first face. The normal of the face defines the Z axis of the matrix and the X and Y axes define the orientation of the decal and must be both perpendicular to the Z axis and to each other. Reversing the direction of the X or Y axis will flip the decal in that direction. The magnitude of the X and Y axes controls the scale and the scale can be non-uniform, meaning the length of the X and Y vectors do not need to be the same.   To set the transform, use the redefine method.

assemblyContext.title: Decal.assemblyContext Property
assemblyContext.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = decal_var.assemblyContext

deriveFeature.title: Decal.deriveFeature Property
deriveFeature.description: Returns the DeriveFeature if this decal is derived from another design. This property returns null if the decal is not derived from another design (i.e. isDerived property returns false).

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = decal_var.deriveFeature

entityToken.description: Returns a token for the Decal object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same Decal. When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = decal_var.entityToken

imageFilename.title: Decal.imageFilename Property
imageFilename.description: Gets and sets the filename of the image used for the decal. When getting this property, the filename returned is the file that was used when the decal was initially created. it's possible the file may no longer exist. When setting this property, it is the full filename to the image to use for the decal. PNG, JPEG, and TIFF files are supported.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = decal_var.imageFilename

# Set the value of the property.
decal_var.imageFilename = propertyValue

isChainFaces.description: Returns if the decal is limited to a specified set of faces or wraps onto all faces in the body. If this property is True, a single face has been specified and the decal can wrap onto other faces of the body. If False, the decal is limited to the set of specified faces. To change this setting, use the redefine method.

# Get the value of the property.

isLightBulbOn.description: Gets and sets if the light bulb of this decal as displayed in the browser is on or off. A decal will only be visible if the light bulb is switched on. However, the light bulb can be on and the decal still invisible if the visibility of a higher level occurrence has its light bulb off or if the light bulb for the Decals folder is off to turn off all decals in a component.

transform.title: Decal.transform Property
transform.description: Gets the transform of the decal. The returned matrix defines the position, rotation, scaling, and flipping. This is done by providing a 3D matrix which defines a 3D coordinate system in model space. The origin of the matrix defines the center of the decal and must lie somewhere on the first face. The normal of the face defines the Z axis of the matrix and the X and Y axes define the orientation of the decal and must be both perpendicular to the Z axis and to each other. Reversing the direction of the X or Y axis will flip the decal in that direction. The magnitude of the X and Y axes controls the scale and the scale can be non-uniform, meaning the length of the X and Y vectors do not need to be the same. To set the transform, use the redefine method.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = decal_var.transform

classType.title: Decal.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.Decal.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::Decal::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: Decal.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = decal_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = decal_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: Decal.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the decal from the component.

syntax.python: returnValue = decal_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = decal_var->deleteMe();

redefine.title: Decal.redefine Method
redefine.description: Redefines the position, orientation, and how the decal is applied to the body. To call this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this decal. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisDecal.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = decal_var.redefine(position, faces, isChainFaces)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = decal_var->redefine(position, faces, isChainFaces);

parameters.name: position
parameters.description: Defines the position, rotation, scaling, and flipping of the decal. The input 3D matrix defines a 3D coordinate system in model space. The origin of the matrix defines the center of the decal and must lie somewhere on the first face. The Z-axis of the matrix should be the same as the normal of the face at the origin. The X and Y axes define the orientation of the decal and must be both perpendicular to the Z axis and each other. Reversing the direction of the X or Y axis will flip the decal in that direction. The magnitude of the X and Y axes controls the scale, and the scale can be non-uniform, meaning the length of the X and Y vectors do not need to be the same.

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: Defines the face where the decal will be placed. The first face in the array is the primary face, which is where the position point must be on. If the isChainFaces argument is true, only the primary face is needed and it will be used to define the position and orientation of the decal and the decal can wrap onto all the faces of the body. If the isChainFaces argument is false, the decal can only be applied to the specified faces with the first face in the array being used as the primary face.

parameters.name: isChainFaces
parameters.description: If true, only one face is needed and the decal wraps onto all the faces of the body. If false, the decal can only be applied to the specified faces with the first face being used to calculate the position and orientation.

saveImage.title: Decal.saveImage Method
saveImage.description: Saves the image associated with the decal to the specified file. This is useful in cases where the original image file is no longer available but you need the image for some other purpose.

syntax.python: returnValue = decal_var.saveImage(filename)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = decal_var->saveImage(filename);

saveImage.return_description: Returns true if writing the file was successful.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The full filename of the image to save, including the extension of the file, which controls what format the image file will be. If file extension is other than png, jpg or tiff, then by default png extension will be added to the filename. This method will fail if a file with the specified filename already exists. If you want to overwrite the file, you'll need to delete it first before calling this method.
